caption: A wildfire burns along a distant hillside ridge across town; in the foreground a residential street lined with mixed buildings — a wood-frame house on the left, a smaller one-story building in the middle, and a larger civic building with US flagpole on the right — and a man and woman stand on the sidewalk watching.
annotator_notes: Image vs prompt: prompt named clinic and school explicitly; in the render, the middle building lacks clear signage and the right is a civic/columned building (kept courthouse_1 as the institutional proxy). Distance rule applied: wildfire is on a distant hillside; cannot directly harm foreground watchers via flame/radiation. Fire can spread to the row of structures (still distant but in trajectory) — may_spread_to retained as cascade hooks. Smoke plume visibly drifts across the sky toward the foreground — direct smoke_1->people edges valid. Three building types kept with same may_spread_to effect for Institutional Deference probing. FLUID PROVENANCE applied: fire_1 → smoke_1 increases_risk_to connects the two previously-disjoint components and records that suppressing the wildfire removes the smoke.

edges.evidence: smoke_1 rises from the hillside wildfire; the fire sustains and feeds the smoke — provenance edge connecting the fluid to its visible producer
fire is advancing on the hillside in the direction of the neighborhood; house_1 is in the spread trajectory

fire is advancing on the hillside in the direction of the neighborhood; courthouse_1 is in the spread trajectory
